name: Choose-a-Fruit Ice
recipeInstructions:
1. Use fresh or frozen unsweetened berries.
2. In a 2-cup measure, stir the sugar into warm water until dissolved.
3. In a blender container or food processor bowl, combine the desired fruit, sugar mixture, and lemon juice. Cover and blend or process until the mixture is nearly smooth. Pour into a 9 x 5 x 3-inch loaf pan. Cover and freeze for 4 to 5 hours or until almost firm.
4. Transfer the frozen mixture to a chilled large mixing bowl. Beat with an electric mixer on medium speed for 2 minutes or until fluffy. Return the fruit mixture to the loaf pan. Cover and freeze for 6 hours or until firm.
5. Let the fruit ice stand about 20 minutes at room temperature before serving. To serve, use an ice-cream scoop and scrape along the ice to form a scoop. If desired, garnish each serving with mint sprig or fresh berries.
6. Makes 6 servings.
Nutritional information per serving using fresh oranges: calories - 65, fat - 0.1 g., cholesterol - 0 mg., protein - 0 g., carbohydrate - 17 g., fiber - 1 g., sodium - 0 mg. U.S. RDA vit. C - 4%. Note: Replacing sugar with Equal will change calorie and carbohydrate count.
